question_text: The service times (in minutes) at two petrol pumps 𝑃1 and 𝑃2 follow distributions with probability density functions 𝑓1(𝑥) = 𝜆𝑒−𝜆𝑥, 𝑥> 0 and 𝑓2(𝑥) = 𝜆2𝑥 𝑒−𝜆𝑥, 𝑥> 0, respectively, where 𝜆> 0. For service, a customer chooses 𝑃1 or 𝑃2 randomly with equal probability. Suppose, the probability that the service time for the customer is more than one minute, is 2𝑒−2. Then the value of 𝜆 equals ____________ (
